# Informatica Business 360 Connector - Full Documentation

# Informatica Business 360 Connector 1.0

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

> [!NOTE] Where possible, we changed noninclusive terms to align with our company value of Equality.
> We maintained certain terms to avoid any effect on customer implementations.

Anypoint Connector for Informatica Business 360 (Informatica Business 360 Connector) enables Mule
applications to integrate with the Informatica Business 360 (MDM) REST API. Use this connector to
manage master data records, perform searches, run ML-based fuzzy matching, and discover business
object metadata, all from within your Mule flows.

For information about compatibility and fixed issues, refer to the Informatica Business 360
Connector Release Notes.

## Before You Begin

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The Informatica Business 360 Connectorâs API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)
- Elements and global elements in a Mule flow
- How to create a Mule app using Anypoint Code Builder or Anypoint Studio

Before creating an app, you must have:

- Credentials to access the Informatica Business 360 target resource
- Anypoint Platform
- The latest versions of Anypoint Code Builder or Anypoint Studio
- Access to an Informatica Business 360 (MDM) environment with valid credentials
- At least one business entity configured in your B360 tenant (for example, `c360.person`)

## Supported Authentication Methods

This connector supports two Informatica API authentication versions:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Method</th><th>Description</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Basic Auth V3</p></td><td><p>Authenticates via the V3 login endpoint (<code>/saas/public/core/v3/login</code>) using JSON. Uses the <code>IDS-SESSION-ID</code> session header.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Basic Auth V2</p></td><td><p>Authenticates via the V2 login endpoint (<code>/ma/api/v2/user/login</code>) using JSON. Uses the <code>icSessionId</code> session header. Retained for environments that require the V2 endpoint.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Key Capabilities

- Entity CRUD Operations

  Create, read, update (full and partial via JSON patch), delete, and restore business entity
  records such as persons, organizations, and custom entity types.

- Search with Automatic Pagination

  Perform full-text or field-based searches across entity records. The connector handles pagination
  transparently, fetching pages on demand as your flow iterates over results.

- ML-Based Fuzzy Matching

  Find similar records using Informaticaâs match engine. Search Match V1 matches against source
  records using blocking fields defined in the match model.

- Metadata Discovery

  The connector dynamically retrieves available business entity types and source systems from the
  B360 tenant. Anypoint Studio and Anypoint Code Builder display dynamic dropdowns for object types
  and source systems.

- Dynamic Input and Output Metadata

  The connector dynamically resolves input and output schemas at design time based on the selected
  business object type, providing field-level autocompletion in Anypoint Studio and Anypoint Code
  Builder. Dynamic input and output metadata for the operation is cached and is automatically
  refreshed once every 10 minutes.

- Automatic Session Management

  The connector manages the session token lifecycle automatically, including proactive refresh
  before the 30-minute expiration window and transparent retry on 401 (Unauthorized) responses.

- Error Handling Hierarchy

  Structured error types (`CLIENT_ERROR`, `SERVER_ERROR`, `CONNECTIVITY`) with specific subtypes
  (`NOT_FOUND`, `BAD_REQUEST`, `RATE_LIMITED`, etc.) enable precise error handling in your flows.

- Debug Logging

  When the connector logger is set to `DEBUG` level, HTTP request and response details are logged
  automatically with sensitive data masked. No separate configuration is required.

## Connection Management

The connector uses a cached connection model (`CachedConnectionProvider`), meaning a single
connection is shared across all operations within a Mule app. The session token is proactively
refreshed on a configurable interval (default: 25 minutes) to avoid mid-request expiration.

If a request receives a 401 (Unauthorized) response, the connector automatically refreshes the
session token and retries the request once before propagating the error.

## Next Step

After you complete the prerequisites, youâre ready to create an app and configure the connector
using [Anypoint Studio](informatica-b360-connector-studio) or
[Anypoint Code Builder](informatica-b360-connector-acb).

## See Also

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)
- [Use Exchange to Discover Connectors, Templates, and Examples](../../connectors/introduction/intro-use-exchange)
- [Informatica Business 360 Connector Reference](informatica-b360-connector-reference)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com)
---

# Using Anypoint Code Builder to Configure Informatica Business 360 Connector 1.0

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

**Open Beta Release**: The **cloud IDE is in open beta**. Any use of Anypoint Code Builder in its
beta state is subject to the applicable beta services terms and conditions, available from the IDE.

To use Anypoint Code Builder to configure a connector or module, create a basic integration project
in Anypoint Code Builder, add connectors and other components to your Mule application to process
your business logic, and configure the attributes.

## Before You Begin

Before creating an integration project, you must:

- [Set up and access the Anypoint Code Builder web or desktop IDE](../../anypoint-code-builder/start-acb)
- Have credentials to access the connectorâs API
- Ensure that the Visual Studio Code Extension Pack for Java is installed.

To use this connector, you must be familiar with:

- The connectorâs API
- Anypoint Connectors
- Mule runtime engine (Mule)

## Create a New Integration Project

To create a new integration project:

1.  In the activity bar of the IDE, click the
    ![Anypoint Code Builder icon](../../connectors/_images/acb-icon-ms-logo.png) (**Anypoint Code
    Builder**) icon.
2.  From **Quick Actions**, click **Develop an Integration**:

    ![Develop an Integration link highlighted in the Getting started section](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/develop-integration-option-mat.png)

3.  Complete the following in the **Develop an Integration** form:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Value</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Project Name</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Unique name for your project.</p></div><div><p>This name is used as the title and name of the integration project file. For example, if the project name is "Salesforce Integration," the project file name is <code>salesforce-integration</code>.</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><div><div><p><strong>Project Location</strong></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Your home directory or another directory you create. Click <strong>Browse</strong> to select a different directory for the integration project.</p></div><div><p>Donât create the project within another project directory.</p></div><div><p>See <a href="../../anypoint-code-builder/start-add-folders">Adding Folders to Your Home Directory</a>.</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

4.  Select the type of project to create by selecting either the **Empty Project** or **Template or
    Example** card.
5.  Select the **Mule runtime** and **Java Version** for your app.
6.  Click **Create Project**.

## Add the Connector to Your Integration Project

Anypoint Connectors provide operations for retrieving, modifying, and sending data to and from
systems.

In addition to the
[built-in connectors](../../anypoint-code-builder/acb-reference#builtin-connectors) that Anypoint
Code Builder provides, you can download many other connectors from Anypoint Exchange.

To import a connector from Exchange and add it to your configuration:

1.  In the Explorer view, open the configuration XML file for your project, such as
    `my-project-name.xml`.
2.  Click the
    ![Show Mule graphical mode icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-tree-view.png) (**Show
    Mule graphical mode**) icon in the activity bar to open the canvas UI if it doesnât open
    automatically.
3.  Add the connector the same way you added other components from the canvas UI:
    1.  In the canvas UI, click the
        ![Add component icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-plus.png) (**Add component**)
        icon.
    2.  In the **Add Component** panel, click **Connectors**.
    3.  Click the connector name and then click the operation to add, such as **Publish**:

        ![Add Publish operation from Anypoint MQ Connector](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/int-add-connector-operation.png)

        If the connector is not available locally, click the
        ![Search in Exchange icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-search-exchange.png)
        (**Search in Exchange**) toggle:

        ![Search in Exchange toggle](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/int-connector-search-exchange.png)

        <table><tbody><tr><td><i data-value="1"></i><b>1</b></td><td>Search locally</td></tr><tr><td><i data-value="2"></i><b>2</b></td><td>Search in Exchange</td></tr></tbody></table>

    4.  Select the connector to add to your project.
    5.  Select the operation from the **Add Component** panel.

Adding a connector to an integration project does not make that connector available to other
projects in your Anypoint Code Builder workspace.

## Configure the Source For the Flow

A trigger (source) starts the flow when specified conditions are met. If a connector has its own
associated sources, you can use a connector-specific trigger to initiate the flow. Each connector
trigger has a unique configuration.

You can configure the following sources for this connector:

- **HTTP > Listener**

  Initiates a flow each time it receives a request on the configured host and port

- **Scheduler**

  Initiates a flow when a time-based condition is met

For example, to configure an HTTP Listener trigger, follow these steps:

1.  Click the **Listener** component on the canvas UI.
2.  (Optional) Click the edit icon to change the name of the **Listener** component.
3.  (Optional) If you created a configuration file for the connector, select it.
4.  In the **General** tab, in the **Path** field, enter the relative path for the path set in the
    HTTP configuration element. See
    [Configure Paths](../../http-connector/latest/http-listener-ref#configure-paths).
5.  Select the **Advanced** tab to configure the following attributes:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Attribute</th><th>Description</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Allowed methods</p></td><td><p>To allow all HTTP methods (default), do not define this attribute. To restrict certain methods, specify all allowed HTTP methods, for example, <code>GET, POST</code>, as a comma-separated list.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response streaming mode</p></td><td><p>Whether to use streaming when the response is sent.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output MIME type</p></td><td><p>MIME type of the payload that this component outputs.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Parameters</p></td><td><p>Appends a key-value pair for a reader property to the value of the Output MIME attribute. Multiple key-value pairs are allowed.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output encoding</p></td><td><p>Encoding of the payload that this component outputs.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><p>Configures the way data streams are consumed in Mule apps.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary node only</p></td><td><p>Select this option to execute the <strong>Listener</strong> trigger on the primary node only when running in a cluster.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery policy</p></td><td><p>Configures the redelivery policy for executing requests that generate errors. You can add a redelivery policy to any source in a flow.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection strategy</p></td><td><p>When an operation in a Mule application fails to connect to an external server, the default behavior is for the operation to fail immediately and return a connectivity error. You can modify the default behavior by configuring the reconnection strategy for the operation.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response</p></td><td><p>See <a href="../../http-connector/latest/http-documentation#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Response</p></td><td><p>HTTP error response returns a status code for the error.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><p>Body of the error message.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Headers</p></td><td><p>Headers to include in the error message.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Status code</p></td><td><p>Status code of the error response, for example, <code>500</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reason phrase</p></td><td><p>Reason for the error, for example, <code>Gateway Timeout</code> or <code>Internal Server Error</code>.</p></td><td><p>No</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Add Components to Your Project

Add components to your integration project to build a flow:

1.  In the **Explorer** view, open the XML file for your project, such as `my-project-name.xml`.
2.  Select **Build a Flow** from the start card to create an empty flow:

    ![Anypoint Code Builder canvas with starting cards with the options to start from scratch, build a flow, or build a subflow](../../connectors/_images/acb-build-flow.png)

3.  Change the name of the flow:
    1.  Click **Flow**.
    2.  Click the edit icon.

        ![Arrow pointing to the flow name edit pencil icon](../../connectors/_images/acb-change-flow-name.png)

    3.  Enter the flow name, and click the checkmark.
    4.  In the canvas UI, click the
        ![Add component icon](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-plus.png) (**Add component**)
        icon.
    5.  In the **Add Component** panel, search for and select your component from the results. The
        following example shows the **Listener** component from the **HTTP** search results:

        ![Listener component highlighted in the Add Component section](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/main-tutorial-add-first-listener.png)

        The configuration XML file now includes the XML for the HTTP Listener into the `<flow/>`
        element, for example:

        ```XML
        <flow name="getFlights" >
          <http:listener path="" config-ref="" doc:name="Listener" doc:id="ojzfry" />
        </flow>
        ```

## Configure the Component Attributes

After you add components to your integration project, configure the attributes in the component UI,
or configure the attributes directly in the XML code editor.

Connector XML tags start with a namespace followed by an operation, following the syntax
`namespace:operation`, such as `<db:select/>` for a **Select** operation in Anypoint Connector for
Database (Database Connector).

You can use autocomplete from an expression (**fx**) field for a component to select DataWeave
functions from the Core module and other values accepted in this field.

### Connection Configuration (V3)

```xml
<informatica-b360:config name="InfaB360_Config">
    <informatica-b360:basic-auth-v3-connection
        baseUrl="https://dmr-us.informaticacloud.com"
        username="${informatica.username}"
        password="${informatica.password}" />
</informatica-b360:config>
```

### Connection Configuration (V2)

```xml
<informatica-b360:config name="InfaB360_Config_V2">
    <informatica-b360:basic-auth-v2-connection
        baseUrl="https://dmr-us.informaticacloud.com"
        username="${informatica.username}"
        password="${informatica.password}" />
</informatica-b360:config>
```

### Connection Configuration with Advanced Settings

```xml
<informatica-b360:config name="InfaB360_Config_Advanced">
    <informatica-b360:basic-auth-v3-connection
        baseUrl="${informatica.baseUrl}"
        username="${informatica.username}"
        password="${informatica.password}"
        connectionTimeout="60"
        connectionTimeoutUnit="SECONDS"
        refreshIntervalMinutes="20"
        usePersistentConnections="true"
        maxConnections="10">
        <reconnection>
            <reconnect frequency="2000" count="3" />
        </reconnection>
    </informatica-b360:basic-auth-v3-connection>
</informatica-b360:config>
```

### Entity Operations

#### Create Entity

```xml
<informatica-b360:create-entity
    config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
    objectType="c360.person"
    sourceSystem="b360.default.system">
    <informatica-b360:payload><![CDATA[#[output application/json ---
    {
        firstName: "John",
        lastName: "Smith"
    }]]]></informatica-b360:payload>
</informatica-b360:create-entity>
```

#### Read Entity

```xml
<informatica-b360:read-entity
    config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
    objectType="c360.person"
    businessId="#[vars.businessId]" />
```

#### Update Entity (Full)

```xml
<informatica-b360:update-entity
    config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
    objectType="c360.person"
    businessId="#[vars.businessId]"
    sourceSystem="b360.default.system">
    <informatica-b360:payload><![CDATA[#[output application/json ---
    {
        firstName: "John",
        lastName: "Doe"
    }]]]></informatica-b360:payload>
</informatica-b360:update-entity>
```

#### Partial Update Entity (JSON Patch)

```xml
<informatica-b360:partial-update-entity
    config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
    objectType="c360.person"
    businessId="#[vars.businessId]"
    sourceSystem="b360.default.system">
    <informatica-b360:update-operations><![CDATA[#[output application/json ---
    [
        { op: "replace", path: "lastName", value: "UpdatedName" }
    ]]]]></informatica-b360:update-operations>
</informatica-b360:partial-update-entity>
```

#### Delete Entity

```xml
<informatica-b360:delete-entity
    config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
    objectType="c360.person"
    businessId="#[vars.businessId]"
    sourceSystem="b360.default.system" />
```

#### Restore Entity

```xml
<informatica-b360:restore-entity
    config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
    objectType="c360.person"
    businessId="#[vars.businessId]"
    sourceSystem="b360.default.system" />
```

### Search Operations

#### Search

```xml
<informatica-b360:search
    config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
    objectType="c360.person"
    pageSize="50">
    <informatica-b360:query><![CDATA[#[output application/json ---
    {
        search: "Smith",
        sort: [{ fieldName: "lastName", order: "ASCENDING" }]
    }]]]></informatica-b360:query>
</informatica-b360:search>
```

#### Search Match V1 (Source Records)

```xml
<informatica-b360:search-match-v1
    config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
    objectType="c360.person">
    <informatica-b360:payload><![CDATA[#[output application/json ---
    {
        searchControls: {
            maxRecordsToReturn: 20,
            searchLevel: "Typical"
        },
        data: {
            searchRecord: {
                fullName: "JOHN SMITH"
            }
        }
    }]]]></informatica-b360:payload>
</informatica-b360:search-match-v1>
```

## Create a New Configuration XML File

Anypoint Code Builder creates the initial Mule configuration XML file in `src/main/mule` within the
Mule project. This file contains global configurations and flow structures.

You can create additional configuration files, for example, to store global configurations.

To create a new XML configuration file:

1.  In the Explorer view, right-click the **mule** folder (`src/main/mule`) and select **New File**.

    Alternatively, use the menu.

    Show me how

    Select the `mule` folder, and then:
    - In the desktop IDE, select **File** > **New Mule Configuration File**.
    - In the cloud IDE, click the ![](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/icon-menu.png) (menu)
      icon, and select **File** > **New File**.

2.  Enter a name for the new file with the `.xml` extension.
3.  In the Editor view for the new file, press Ctrl+Space to display available options.
4.  Select **Empty mule configuration**:

    ![New Mule configuration option in auto-correct](../../anypoint-code-builder/_images/int-new-mule-config.png)

    This command adds the following code to the file:

    ```xml
    <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
    <mule xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation"
        xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
        xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance"
        xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
        http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd">

    </mule>
    ```

# Informatica Business 360 Connector 1.0 Examples

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

This page provides examples for Informatica Business 360 Connector operations. Each example shows
the XML configuration you can use in Anypoint Studio or a text editor.

## Before You Begin

- Access to an Informatica Business 360 (MDM) environment with valid credentials
- A Mule project with Informatica Business 360 Connector added as a dependency
- A configured global element for the connector (see
  [Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Informatica Business 360 Connector 1.0](informatica-b360-connector-studio))

## Create and Read a Business Entity

This example creates a new person record and then reads it back using the returned business ID.

### XML Configuration

```xml
<flow name="create-and-read-entity-flow">
    <scheduler>
        <scheduling-strategy>
            <fixed-frequency frequency="60" timeUnit="SECONDS" />
        </scheduling-strategy>
    </scheduler>

    <!-- Create a new person record -->
    <informatica-b360:create-entity
        config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
        objectType="c360.person"
        sourceSystem="b360.default.system">
        <informatica-b360:payload><![CDATA[#[output application/json ---
        {
            firstName: "Jane",
            lastName: "Smith"
        }]]]></informatica-b360:payload>
    </informatica-b360:create-entity>

    <set-variable variableName="businessId" value="#[payload.businessId]" />
    <logger level="INFO" message="Created entity with businessId: #[vars.businessId]" />

    <!-- Read the created record -->
    <informatica-b360:read-entity
        config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
        objectType="c360.person"
        businessId="#[vars.businessId]" />

    <logger level="INFO" message="Read entity: #[payload]" />
</flow>
```

## Update and Partial Update a Record

This example shows both a full update (PUT) and a field-level partial update (PATCH) using JSON
Patch format.

### XML Configuration

```xml
<flow name="update-entity-flow">
    <http:listener config-ref="HTTP_Listener_Config" path="/update" />

    <!-- Full update: all mandatory fields must be included -->
    <informatica-b360:update-entity
        config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
        objectType="c360.person"
        businessId="#[attributes.queryParams.businessId]"
        sourceSystem="b360.default.system">
        <informatica-b360:payload><![CDATA[#[output application/json ---
        {
            firstName: "Jane",
            lastName: "Doe"
        }]]]></informatica-b360:payload>
    </informatica-b360:update-entity>

    <logger level="INFO" message="Full update result: #[payload]" />

    <!-- Partial update: only change the lastName field -->
    <informatica-b360:partial-update-entity
        config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
        objectType="c360.person"
        businessId="#[attributes.queryParams.businessId]"
        sourceSystem="b360.default.system">
        <informatica-b360:update-operations><![CDATA[#[output application/json ---
        [
            {
                op: "replace",
                path: "lastName",
                value: "UpdatedName"
            }
        ]]]]></informatica-b360:update-operations>
    </informatica-b360:partial-update-entity>

    <logger level="INFO" message="Partial update result: #[payload]" />
</flow>
```

## Delete and Restore a Record

This example soft-deletes a record and then restores it.

### XML Configuration

```xml
<flow name="delete-and-restore-flow">
    <http:listener config-ref="HTTP_Listener_Config" path="/delete-restore" />

    <!-- Soft-delete the record -->
    <informatica-b360:delete-entity
        config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
        objectType="c360.person"
        businessId="#[attributes.queryParams.businessId]"
        sourceSystem="b360.default.system" />

    <logger level="INFO" message="Delete result: #[payload]" />

    <!-- Restore the soft-deleted record -->
    <informatica-b360:restore-entity
        config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
        objectType="c360.person"
        businessId="#[attributes.queryParams.businessId]"
        sourceSystem="b360.default.system" />

    <logger level="INFO" message="Restore result: #[payload]" />
</flow>
```

## Search with Pagination

This example performs a full-text search and iterates over paginated results.

### XML Configuration

```xml
<flow name="search-flow">
    <http:listener config-ref="HTTP_Listener_Config" path="/search" />

    <informatica-b360:search
        config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
        objectType="c360.person"
        pageSize="50"
        recordOffset="0"
        recordsToReturn="100">
        <informatica-b360:query><![CDATA[#[output application/json ---
        {
            search: "*",
            sort: [
                { fieldName: "lastName", order: "ASCENDING" }
            ]
        }]]]></informatica-b360:query>
    </informatica-b360:search>

    <!-- Results are automatically paginated â iterate over them -->
    <foreach>
        <logger level="INFO" message="Search result: #[payload]" />
    </foreach>
</flow>
```

## Fuzzy Matching with Search Match V1

This example performs ML-based fuzzy matching against source records to find similar person records.

### XML Configuration

```xml
<flow name="search-match-v1-flow">
    <http:listener config-ref="HTTP_Listener_Config" path="/match-v1" />

    <informatica-b360:search-match-v1
        config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
        objectType="c360.person">
        <informatica-b360:payload><![CDATA[#[output application/json ---
        {
            searchControls: {
                maxRecordsToReturn: 20,
                searchLevel: "Typical",
                fileRecordLimit: 1000,
                matchPreference: "BEST_SCORE"
            },
            data: {
                searchRecord: {
                    fullName: "JANE SMITH"
                }
            }
        }]]]></informatica-b360:payload>
    </informatica-b360:search-match-v1>

    <logger level="INFO" message="Match V1 results: #[payload]" />
</flow>
```

## Error Handling

This example shows how to handle specific connector error types in your flows.

### XML Configuration

```xml
<flow name="error-handling-flow">
    <http:listener config-ref="HTTP_Listener_Config" path="/read" />

    <try>
        <informatica-b360:read-entity
            config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
            objectType="c360.person"
            businessId="#[attributes.queryParams.businessId]" />
        <error-handler>
            <on-error-continue type="INFORMATICA-B360:NOT_FOUND">
                <set-payload value='#[output application/json --- { error: "Record not found" }]' />
            </on-error-continue>
            <on-error-continue type="INFORMATICA-B360:RATE_LIMITED">
                <logger level="WARN" message="Rate limited. Consider adding retry logic" />
                <set-payload value='#[output application/json --- { error: "Rate limited, try again later" }]' />
            </on-error-continue>
            <on-error-propagate type="INFORMATICA-B360:SERVER_ERROR">
                <logger level="ERROR" message="Server error: #[error.description]" />
            </on-error-propagate>
        </error-handler>
    </try>
</flow>
```

## Retry with Until Successful

This example wraps a connector operation with `until-successful` for automatic retry on transient
failures.

### XML Configuration

```xml
<flow name="retry-flow">
    <http:listener config-ref="HTTP_Listener_Config" path="/read-with-retry" />

    <until-successful maxRetries="3" millisBetweenRetries="2000">
        <informatica-b360:read-entity
            config-ref="InfaB360_Config"
            objectType="c360.person"
            businessId="#[attributes.queryParams.businessId]" />
    </until-successful>

    <logger level="INFO" message="Entity: #[payload]" />
</flow>
```

## See Also

- [Informatica Business 360 Connector Reference](informatica-b360-connector-reference)
- [Using Anypoint Studio to Configure Informatica Business 360 Connector](informatica-b360-connector-studio)
- [Salesforce Help](https://help.salesforce.com/s/)
---

# Informatica Business 360 Connector 1.0 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

> [!NOTE] Where possible, we changed noninclusive terms to align with our company value of Equality.
> We maintained certain terms to avoid any effect on customer implementations.

## Configurations

### Default Configuration

Default configuration for the connector.

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Config_BasicAuthV2">Login V2 Connection Provider</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Config_BasicAuthV3">Login V3 Connection Provider</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The identifier of this element used to reference it in other components.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the platform expires the instance at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### Login V2 Connection Provider

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Base URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Base URL for the Informatica MDM API.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The username used to authenticate requests.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to authenticate requests.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Connection timeout for the request.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to use persistent HTTP connections for outbound requests.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum simultaneous outbound connections, unlimited by default.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Time a connection can stay idle before closing. Applies only when persistent connections are enabled.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Idle Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>When enabled, allows processing to begin after headers are parsed. The response must be read eventually to avoid memory issues.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Space in bytes for the buffer where the HTTP response is stored.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Token Refresh Interval (minutes)</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Interval in minutes at which the session token is refreshed.</p></td><td><p>25</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Default">Default</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Ntlm">NTLM</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>HTTP proxy configuration for outbound requests.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When using HTTPS, the TLS configuration used to secure the connection.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in every request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultQueryParam">Default Query Param</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in every request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test does not pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### Login V3 Connection Provider

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Base URL</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Base URL for the Informatica MDM API.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Username</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The username used to authenticate requests.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Password</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The password used to authenticate requests.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Connection timeout for the request.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to use persistent HTTP connections for outbound requests.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum simultaneous outbound connections, unlimited by default.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Time a connection can stay idle before closing. Applies only when persistent connections are enabled.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Connection Idle Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>When enabled, allows processing to begin after headers are parsed. The response must be read eventually to avoid memory issues.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Space in bytes for the buffer where the HTTP response is stored.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Token Refresh Interval (minutes)</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Interval in minutes at which the session token is refreshed.</p></td><td><p>25</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p>One of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Default">Default</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Ntlm">NTLM</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>HTTP proxy configuration for outbound requests.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When using HTTPS, the TLS configuration used to secure the connection.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in every request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Default Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultQueryParam">Default Query Param</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in every request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test does not pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Supported Operations

- [Create Entity](#CreateEntity)
- [Delete Entity](#DeleteEntity)
- [Partial Update Entity](#PartialUpdateEntity)
- [Read Entity](#ReadEntity)
- [Restore Entity](#RestoreEntity)
- [Search](#Search)
- [Search Match V1](#SearchMatchV1)
- [Update Entity](#UpdateEntity)

## Operations

## Create Entity

`<informatica-b360:create-entity>`

Creates a master data record in Informatica Business 360.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Business entity or object type identifier (for example, <code>c360.person</code>).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Source System</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Internal ID of the source system.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Payload</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Request body for the create operation.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Source Primary Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Primary key of the source record.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional query parameters to send with the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional HTTP headers to send with the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- INFORMATICA-B360:BAD_GATEWAY
- INFORMATICA-B360:BAD_REQUEST
- INFORMATICA-B360:CLIENT_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:CONNECTIVITY
- INFORMATICA-B360:FORBIDDEN
- INFORMATICA-B360:GATEWAY_TIMEOUT
- INFORMATICA-B360:NOT_FOUND
- INFORMATICA-B360:RATE_LIMITED
- INFORMATICA-B360:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- INFORMATICA-B360:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVER_BUSY
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVER_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVICE_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:UNAUTHORIZED

## Delete Entity

`<informatica-b360:delete-entity>`

Removes data from Business 360.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Business entity or object type identifier (for example, <code>c360.person</code>).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Business ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique business identifier of the master record.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Source System</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Internal ID of the source system.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Source Primary Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>When specified, deletes only the source record instead of the master record.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional query parameters to send with the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional HTTP headers to send with the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- INFORMATICA-B360:BAD_GATEWAY
- INFORMATICA-B360:BAD_REQUEST
- INFORMATICA-B360:CLIENT_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:CONNECTIVITY
- INFORMATICA-B360:FORBIDDEN
- INFORMATICA-B360:GATEWAY_TIMEOUT
- INFORMATICA-B360:NOT_FOUND
- INFORMATICA-B360:RATE_LIMITED
- INFORMATICA-B360:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- INFORMATICA-B360:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVER_BUSY
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVER_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVICE_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:UNAUTHORIZED

## Partial Update Entity

`<informatica-b360:partial-update-entity>`

Applies partial changes to an existing master record using a list of update operations (for example,
JSON patch), without sending a full entity replacement.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Business entity or object type identifier (for example, <code>c360.person</code>).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Business ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique business identifier of the master record.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Source System</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Internal ID of the source system.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Update Operations</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Partial update operations to apply to the entity (for example, JSON patch operations).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resolve Crosswalk</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Standardize picklist values based on source system configuration.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional query parameters to send with the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional HTTP headers to send with the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- INFORMATICA-B360:BAD_GATEWAY
- INFORMATICA-B360:BAD_REQUEST
- INFORMATICA-B360:CLIENT_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:CONNECTIVITY
- INFORMATICA-B360:FORBIDDEN
- INFORMATICA-B360:GATEWAY_TIMEOUT
- INFORMATICA-B360:NOT_FOUND
- INFORMATICA-B360:RATE_LIMITED
- INFORMATICA-B360:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- INFORMATICA-B360:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVER_BUSY
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVER_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVICE_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:UNAUTHORIZED

## Read Entity

`<informatica-b360:read-entity>`

Fetches a master record.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Business entity or object type identifier (for example, <code>c360.person</code>).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Business ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique business identifier of the master record.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Skip Lookup</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Retrieve picklist values from cache instead of the data store.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Show Content Metadata</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Include trust scores and survivorship configuration.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Show Pending</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Retrieve only master records pending review.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional query parameters to send with the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional HTTP headers to send with the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- INFORMATICA-B360:BAD_GATEWAY
- INFORMATICA-B360:BAD_REQUEST
- INFORMATICA-B360:CLIENT_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:CONNECTIVITY
- INFORMATICA-B360:FORBIDDEN
- INFORMATICA-B360:GATEWAY_TIMEOUT
- INFORMATICA-B360:NOT_FOUND
- INFORMATICA-B360:RATE_LIMITED
- INFORMATICA-B360:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- INFORMATICA-B360:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVER_BUSY
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVER_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVICE_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:UNAUTHORIZED

## Restore Entity

`<informatica-b360:restore-entity>`

Recovers a master record that was previously deleted.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Business entity or object type identifier (for example, <code>c360.person</code>).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Business ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique business identifier of the deleted master record.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Source System</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Internal ID of the source system.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional query parameters to send with the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional HTTP headers to send with the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- INFORMATICA-B360:BAD_GATEWAY
- INFORMATICA-B360:BAD_REQUEST
- INFORMATICA-B360:CLIENT_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:CONNECTIVITY
- INFORMATICA-B360:FORBIDDEN
- INFORMATICA-B360:GATEWAY_TIMEOUT
- INFORMATICA-B360:NOT_FOUND
- INFORMATICA-B360:RATE_LIMITED
- INFORMATICA-B360:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- INFORMATICA-B360:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVER_BUSY
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVER_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVICE_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:UNAUTHORIZED

## Search

`<informatica-b360:search>`

Runs a search against a business entity type.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Business entity GUID (for example, <code>c360.person</code>).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Search query body in JSON format with <code>search</code> or <code>fields</code> parameters.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records per page (maximum 10000).</p></td><td><p>50</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Record Offset</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Number of records to skip before returning results.</p></td><td><p>0</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Records To Return</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Total number of records to return (<code>-1</code> for all matching records).</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes iterable results. Repeatable iteration is the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional query parameters to send with the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional HTTP headers to send with the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- INFORMATICA-B360:BAD_GATEWAY
- INFORMATICA-B360:BAD_REQUEST
- INFORMATICA-B360:CLIENT_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:CONNECTIVITY
- INFORMATICA-B360:FORBIDDEN
- INFORMATICA-B360:GATEWAY_TIMEOUT
- INFORMATICA-B360:NOT_FOUND
- INFORMATICA-B360:RATE_LIMITED
- INFORMATICA-B360:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- INFORMATICA-B360:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVER_BUSY
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVER_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVICE_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:UNAUTHORIZED

## Search Match V1

`<informatica-b360:search-match-v1>`

Invokes Informatica Business 360 match (fuzzy matching) for a record against a published match model
on the selected object type.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Business entity GUID for an entity with a published match model (for example, <code>c360.person</code>).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Payload</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>JSON body with <code>searchControls</code> and <code>data.searchRecord</code>.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Match Model</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique identifier of a published match model.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Version</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Match model version number (defaults to the latest version).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional query parameters to send with the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional HTTP headers to send with the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- INFORMATICA-B360:BAD_GATEWAY
- INFORMATICA-B360:BAD_REQUEST
- INFORMATICA-B360:CLIENT_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:CONNECTIVITY
- INFORMATICA-B360:FORBIDDEN
- INFORMATICA-B360:GATEWAY_TIMEOUT
- INFORMATICA-B360:NOT_FOUND
- INFORMATICA-B360:RATE_LIMITED
- INFORMATICA-B360:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- INFORMATICA-B360:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVER_BUSY
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVER_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVICE_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:UNAUTHORIZED

## Update Entity

`<informatica-b360:update-entity>`

Replaces an existing master record with the full payload.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Business entity or object type identifier (for example, <code>c360.person</code>).</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Business ID</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Unique business identifier of the master record.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Source System</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Internal ID of the source system.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Payload</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Request body for the update operation.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resolve Crosswalk</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Standardize picklist values based on source system configuration.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional query parameters to send with the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional HTTP headers to send with the request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An expression to evaluate against the operationâs output and store the expression outcome in the target variable.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Error Mappings</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#ErrorMapping">Error Mapping</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Set of error mappings.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">HTTP Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Default Configuration](#Config)

### Throws

- INFORMATICA-B360:BAD_GATEWAY
- INFORMATICA-B360:BAD_REQUEST
- INFORMATICA-B360:CLIENT_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:CONNECTIVITY
- INFORMATICA-B360:FORBIDDEN
- INFORMATICA-B360:GATEWAY_TIMEOUT
- INFORMATICA-B360:NOT_FOUND
- INFORMATICA-B360:RATE_LIMITED
- INFORMATICA-B360:REQUEST_TIMEOUT
- INFORMATICA-B360:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVER_BUSY
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVER_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:SERVICE_ERROR
- INFORMATICA-B360:UNAUTHORIZED

## Types

### TLS

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Protocols</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A comma-separated list of protocols enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Enabled Cipher Suites</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>A comma-separated list of cipher suites enabled for this context.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Trust Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#TrustStore">Trust Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Trust store configuration for verifying server certificates.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Store</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#KeyStore">Key Store</a></p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Key store configuration for client certificate authentication.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Revocation Check</strong></p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#StandardRevocationCheck">Standard Revocation Check</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CustomOcspResponder">Custom OCSP Responder</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#CrlFile">CRL File</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Certificate revocation checking configuration.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Trust Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of store used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The algorithm used by the trust store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Insecure</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>If true, no certificate validations will be performed, rendering connections vulnerable to attacks. Use at your own risk.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Key Store

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Path</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The location (which will be resolved relative to the current classpath and file system, if possible) of the key store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of store used.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Alias</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>When the key store contains many private keys, this attribute indicates the alias of the key that should be used. If not defined, the first key in the file will be used by default.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Key Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the private key.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Password</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The password used to protect the key store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Algorithm</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The algorithm used by the key store.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Standard Revocation Check

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Field</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Only End Entities</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Only verify the last element of the certificate chain.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Prefer Crls</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Try CRL instead of OCSP first.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>No Fallback</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Do not use the secondary checking method (the one not selected before).</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Soft Fail</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Avoid verification failure when the revocation server can not be reached or is busy.</p></div></div></td><td><div></div></td><td><div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Custom OCSP Responder
